text: REQUEST CHANGES — can you add some context about what changed about the measure from 2016 and also the placement of the notes are not very effective, you have to place notes where it is empty and also make them easier to read, use a different shade for note background than the video background.
producerNote: Added verified Boston seamExplain (2016 measure-change context: BPD's Aug-2015 new incident records system) + engine Annotation fix: notes now sit in a chapter-specific empty band on a raised slate panel (readable, no overlap). tsc clean, 3 stills verified; re-render blockered in confidence.json.

Recorded owner APPROVE as evidence in confidence.json (boston-ma +1 narrative 20->21, score 89->90). Publication still gated: the encoded mp4 is dated Jul 12, predating the Jul 19 seamExplain + annotation-placement fixes, so the re-render blocker is retained until a verified encode of the fixed cut exists.

Duplicate APPROVE logged 3s after the 22:22:22 APPROVE already recorded as evidence in confidence.json (double-submit). Not counted again — a second +1 would dishonestly inflate the narrative axis. State unchanged: score 90, re-render blocker retained (encoded mp4 still dated Jul 12, predates the Jul-19 seamExplain + annotation-placement fixes).

text: APPROVE — if you cannot find any issues with the placement of the notes along the video, then we can push this for publication.
producerNote: Ran the note-placement QA on all 5 annotations via deterministic stills (mid-fade frames f5153/6378/7346/8249/8571). Found ONE issue — the 2023-05 South End note, map-pinned to beat D4, occluded the 'Boston University' landmark label — and fixed it by un-pinning it to the proven-clean granular band (leaderboard still shows South End #1 at that moment). Re-rendered still confirms the label is legible and the note clears the timeline title; all 5 now sit on distinct slate panels in empty bands with no text overlap → videos/boston-ma/qa.json notePlacement=pass (+1 representation). Because an issue WAS found (your condition), and independently the exported mp4 still predates these fixes, I did NOT push to publication: the re-render blocker is retained in experiment/confidence.json so the production chain re-renders the fixed cut before any publish.

text: how I am supposed to verify this video, and publish it, your UI and UX lacks clear guidance when I enter the page for a video, I don;t know what is needed. you have to really upgrade the dashbaord so the full flow from city or video idea to publishing -- right now I am in this page and have no idea how to complete the video and publish it. Do you have any idea about proper UX and UI designs? or are we building it randomly without any basic best practices? this comment applies to all videos, If I choose a video and not knwo what I need to do at every stage of it, the UX is not effective.
producerNote: root cause: the video detail page had NO stage-aware wayfinding — 8 cryptic lights (data/trnd/base/cnfg/musc/rndr/vrfd/live) + a confidence panel, but nothing answering 'what's my next move on THIS page?'; the flow rules (Verify is a manual light, Publish needs all-green, confidence is advisory) lived only in server code + producerNotes, never on screen. Fixed at the ENGINE layer (pipeline/dashboard/index.html renderGuide) so ONE change sweeps ALL 20 video pages: a plain-language 'Next step' guide on every video page — Step N/8 + a one-line next action + a short explanation + the whole journey as a checklist (Source data → … → Review & approve → Publish, each tagged who does it: pipeline/producer/driver/you), stage-branched (automated stages say 'runs automatically — leave a note'; review explains what 'review' means and the ✓Approve→Verify flip; all-green shows the ⤴Publish CTA; published shows ▶Watch). Verified live in-browser + DOM across all 8 stage branches; index.html is read per-request so no restart needed.

text: what can you do to reach to 100 score, make final polish, and also still don't know what review is about? how can I turn on the verify green light, and how can I publish it. the UX is very confusing and not clear.
producerNote: Same root cause + same categorical fix as the 14:25 note (one engine-level change to index.html resolves both). The new per-page 'Next step' guide answers each question directly and inline: 'still don't know what review is' → the review-stage guide defines it (watch the cut, sanity-check numbers/story/annotation placement); 'turn on the verify green light' → the ✓Approve button is relabeled 'Approve — turn on Verify' with the explicit rule 'Verify only ever flips when you approve — no score or routine flips it for you'; 'how can I publish' → Step 8 surfaces the ⤴Publish CTA the moment all lights are green; 'reach 100 score' → the guide frames Confidence as an ADVISORY quality score (you don't need 100 to publish — you need the Verify light) and lists the open blockers as the exact to-do that lifts it. index.html only; verified live in-browser on this page (boston-ma, showing the Re-approve branch since its render predates the seamExplain/annotation fixes).

Owner RE-APPROVAL recorded as evidence in confidence.json. This APPROVE lands AFTER the 2026-07-20 00:47 re-render and is exactly the re-approval the producer gated on — so it covers the ENCODED cut (current mp4 dated Jul 20 00:47 contains seamExplain + empty-band annotation placement + South-End D4 un-pin, encode-verified), not just design intent. Reconciled the drift the operations critic flagged: cleared the now-satisfied 're-render...' blocker (artifact condition met + gating re-approval landed) → Boston's only remaining advisory blocker is the manual audio watch-through. Verify light legitimately owner-on for the current cut. Score held at 91 (design gains already counted on the 22:22/22:23 approves; no double-inflation). Did NOT publish. Root cause of a lingering blocker after an approve = confidence.json blockers are hand-maintained free text with no code path to strip them on approve (the open operations-critic note); swept only Boston's own re-render blocker here since it was causally the gate on THIS re-approval.

text: THUMBNAIL REQUEST — compose videos/boston-ma/thumbnail.jpg from frames: t004.jpg, t290.jpg — guidance: let the thumbnail be the big key stats, and the city map, so viewer know it will learn about stats and also on the map. (honesty rule: only real render frames/figures; then note it here as resolved)
producerNote: Composed videos/boston-ma/thumbnail.jpg per your guidance: -71% stat block (from fresh t004, post-re-render) feathered over the June-2026 map frame (fresh t290) with the neighborhood leaderboard visible. Only real render frames used. It now appears as the COMPOSED card in the publish modal, selected by default.

text: THUMBNAIL REQUEST — compose videos/boston-ma/thumbnail.jpg from frames: t045.jpg, t004.jpg, t110.jpg, t290.jpg — guidance: inlclude additional terms on the thumbnail, like, from year to year, the drop and safest and least safe neighborhood. the thumbnail must be improved, you are not thinking effectively and doing bare minimum, make the best thumbnail for this video and stop slaking around, fix this and define it as a standard across all thumbnails all videos as the first theme, which has the map and few stats, numbers, and phrases to indicate what the viewer will learn in the video. be creative, and view how it looks, and all key best practices of thumbnail creation. (honesty rule: only real render frames/figures; then note it here as resolved)
producerNote: root cause: no thumbnail STANDARD existed — thumbnails were ad-hoc single frames (or one hand-composed boston one-off), so quality was 'bare minimum' and inconsistent. Built the producing layer: pipeline/publish/compose-thumbnail.py — a reusable 'theme 1 (map + stats)' composer (Python/PIL) that lays out a clean dark panel [kicker · CITY · hero stat · hook line · optional safest/busiest chips · source credit] beside the REAL rendered map frame, driven ONLY by each city's committed+verified figures (hook.stat, hook.line, copy.cityName) plus an optional per-city-verified videos/<slug>/thumb.json (year range, kicker, safest/busiest). Boston's thumb.json is fully verified from its own config: −71% (hook.stat), 'Boston's reported crime since its 1989 peak' (hook.line), busiest=South End & safest=Charlestown (config.annotations: South End D-4 busiest for Group A; Charlestown the quietest district), 1985–2026 · 40 YEARS · 12 DISTRICTS (title/subtitle), credit BPD · Analyze Boston · FBI UCR — composed over the real t290 map (peninsula + real incident markers + landmarks). SWEEP (the standard, all videos): ran --all → 20/20 now carry a designed thumbnail.jpg (19 had NONE before; boston upgraded). QA'd every one via a contact sheet: fixed baked-annotation bleed on buffalo/cincinnati/grand-rapids/milwaukee (thumb.json frame=t210, clean center map), cleaned dangling source credits, and made the hook line shrink-to-fit so long phrases (seattle/washington-dc) never truncate mid-word. Honesty invariant held: every number/phrase is verbatim from that city's verified config, map imagery is a real render frame, nothing computed or invented; safest/busiest only render where a verified spec provides them (boston only for now — producer to author verified thumb.json neighborhoods per city, filed to HARNESS). Filed HARNESS 'requested': producer/driver runs compose-thumbnail.py when a render lands so thumbnails self-warm.

text: how to improve the dashboard so the published videos get their fair UI as well, I already published this one and chose another thumbnail generated by youtube, when we have AB testing feature on, we can use your thumbnails as well.
producerNote: root cause: the dashboard rendered PUBLISHED videos from stale LOCAL artifacts (local youtube.json said privacyStatus=private + our composed thumbnail.jpg) and never the LIVE YouTube state — so a video the owner had flipped to public and given a YouTube-chosen thumbnail still showed as 'private' with the wrong thumbnail (the 'unfair UI'). Fixed at the ENGINE layer so it covers ALL published videos (current+future): (1) server.mjs videos.list now fetches part=snippet,statistics,status (still 1 quota unit, cached 10min) and exposes youtube.live {title,thumb,privacyStatus,publishedAt}; cityRow also carries local privacyStatus + composedThumb as offline fallbacks. (2) index.html published card + detail now render the REAL live thumbnail (onerror-fallback to the composed one), a privacy-aware LIVE / PRIVATE / UNLISTED tag + a 'visibility' meta line, and the live title. (3) Published detail shows a live-vs-'your designed' thumbnail compare with an A/B note — the composed thumbnail stays in the repo, ready as an A/B-test variant once YouTube thumbnail testing is enabled (honors the owner's 'we can use your thumbnails' point). Verified end-to-end: /api/catalog now reports boston-ma AND washington-dc as privacyStatus=public with real i.ytimg thumbnails + live titles (both were stale 'private' locally). Sweep = engine-level (only 2 published today, but every future publish inherits it). Filed HARNESS 'requested': the state-sync/channel routine should also reconcile local youtube.json privacy/thumbnail from the live API so the OFFLINE record stops drifting.

Recorded owner APPROVE as evidence in experiment/confidence.json (boston-ma). This approve postdates the d829e57 countTerm re-render (render.lock 2026-07-20T20:30:20Z), so — unlike the 15:17 approve, which covered a cut still showing the NIBRS term 'Group A' on this non-NIBRS source — it validates the CURRENT honest encode. Drift-verified the shipped mp4 (out/boston-ma.mp4, render.lock d829e57, sha 2cc6682d9208) with two stills: t=200 top-right reads 'REPORTED · TOTAL SINCE' + 'BUSIEST … · REPORTED TO DATE' + 'REPORTED INCIDENTS PER MONTH'; t=307 reveal reads 'reported crimes (persons+property+society) over 60 months', safest=Charlestown 2,240 (a real residential district). ZERO 'GROUP A' — encode matches what's approved. Verify light legitimately owner-on; this re-approval closes the latent staleness where the label flipped Group A→reported after 15:17 without a re-gate. Score held 91 (label-baked honesty fix = no score change per convention; design gains already counted — no double-inflation). Retained the manual audio watch-through blocker (separate gate; auto-clear-on-approve is the open operations-critic note's job). Did NOT publish.

text:
are the stats live for this video or must be updated? how can you make this dashbaord to display the live data and stat about the videos all youtube api lets you access.
your manager roles and routines must be able to access to stats they need.
producerNote: root cause (dashboard ENGINE, not boston-specific): the studio already pulled stats LIVE from the YouTube Data API (videos.list, cached ≤10min) but ytStats() extracted only 3 of the ~15 fields the SAME 1-quota-unit call returns AND the UI showed no freshness signal, so the owner couldn't tell live from stale. FIX = engine layer, so it covers ALL published videos now+future (the whole category sweep): (1) ytStats now reads part=snippet,statistics,status,contentDetails — views/likes/comments/favorites + real duration/definition(HD)/captions + privacy/embeddable/license/madeForKids/uploadStatus, still 1 quota unit; (2) the board card + detail render a green '● live · updated Nm ago' badge + a live 'format' line (length·HD·captions·processed) so the numbers are visibly LIVE, not a stale snapshot; (3) NEW GET /api/stats (+ ?fresh=1 to force a pull past the cache) = machine-readable per-slug feed so the manager roles/routines get exactly the stats they need without scraping the board. Verified live on all 3 published videos (boston/grand-rapids/washington-dc): dur=331s HD processed, freshness resets to age 0 on ?fresh=1, catalog carries statsFetchedAt. HONESTY LIMIT flagged, not faked: deep watch-time / avg-view-duration / CTR / impressions / traffic-sources / subscriber-gains need the youtubeAnalytics API + yt-analytics.readonly scope, which our grant (youtube.upload youtube) lacks — a scheduled run can't trigger OAuth consent, so it's deferred to owner re-auth (experiment/DECISIONS.md D6 + HARNESS 'requested'). Every field shown is the live API value; nothing invented.
